documentation.value:
```scala
val name: Type = value
```
Declares an immutable binding.

```scala
var name: Type = value
```
Declares a mutable variable.

```scala
if (cond) expr1 else expr2
```
Alternative branch in conditional expression.

```scala
class Child extends Parent
```
Specifies superclass or trait.
Boolean literal representing the false value.

```scala
final class Sealed
final val x = 42
```
Prevents overriding or subclassing.

```scala
try { ... } finally { cleanup() }
```
Executes cleanup code regardless of exceptions.

```scala
for (x <- xs) yield x * 2
for {
  x <- xs
  y <- ys
} yield (x, y)
```
For loop or for-comprehension.

```scala
List[T] forSome { type T }
```
Existential type quantifier (Scala 2).

```scala
if (condition) expr1 else expr2
```
Conditional expression. Returns a value.

```scala
implicit val ord: Ordering[Int] = ...
implicit def convert(x: A): B = ...
```
Marks a value, method, or class as implicit.

```scala
import scala.collection.mutable.ListBuffer
import java.util.{List, Map}
```
Imports packages, classes, or members.

```scala
lazy val expensive = computeValue()
```
Defers evaluation until first access.

```scala
x match {
  case Some(v) => v
  case None => default
}
```
Pattern matching expression.

```scala
new MyClass(args)
```
Creates a new instance.
The null reference. Use Option instead when possible.

```scala
override def toString: String = ...
```
Overrides a member from a parent class or trait.

```scala
package com.example.myapp
```
Declares the package namespace.

```scala
private val secret = 42
private[this] val instanceOnly = 1
```
Restricts access to the enclosing class.

```scala
protected def helper(): Unit = ...
```
Restricts access to the class and its subclasses.

```scala
def f(): Int = { return 42 }
```
Explicitly returns from a method. Rarely needed in Scala.

```scala
sealed trait Shape
case class Circle(r: Double) extends Shape
```
Restricts subtypes to the same file for exhaustive matching.

```scala
super.method()
super[TraitName].method()
```
Refers to the parent class or trait.

```scala
this.field
def this(x: Int) = this(x, 0)
```
Refers to the current instance.

```scala
throw new IllegalArgumentException("msg")
```
Throws an exception.
Boolean literal representing the true value.

```scala
type Name = String
type F[A] = List[A]
```
Defines a type alias or abstract type member.

```scala
class A extends B with C with D
```
Mixes in additional traits.

```scala
for (x <- xs) yield x * 2
```
Produces a value in a for-comprehension.

```scala
given ord: Ordering[Int] = Ordering.Int
given Ordering[Int] with { ... }
```
Declares a given instance for contextual abstraction.

```scala
def f(using ord: Ordering[Int]): Unit = ...
```
Declares contextual parameters.

```scala
export myObj.{member1, member2}
```
Re-exports members from another object.

```scala
if condition then expr1 else expr2
```
Optional keyword after if condition in Scala 3.

```scala
def longMethod =
  ...
end longMethod
```
Optional end marker for long definitions.

```scala
extension (s: String)
  def greet: String = s"Hello, $s"
```
Adds methods to existing types.

```scala
transparent inline def f: Any = ...
```
Allows the compiler to specialize the return type.

```scala
inline def power(x: Double, inline n: Int): Double = ...
```
Inlines the method at call sites.

```scala
opaque type UserId = Int
```
Defines a type alias whose underlying type is hidden outside its scope.

```scala
open class Base
```
Explicitly allows a class to be extended.

```scala
case class Point(x: Int, y: Int) derives Eq, Show
```
Automatically derives type class instances.

```scala
println("Hello, World!")
println(s"Value: $x")
```
Prints a value followed by a newline to stdout.

```scala
print("Hello")
```
Prints a value to stdout without a trailing newline.

```scala
require(x > 0, "x must be positive")
```
Throws IllegalArgumentException if condition is false.

```scala
assert(list.nonEmpty, "list must not be empty")
```
Throws AssertionError if condition is false.

```scala
sys.exit(0)
```
Terminates the JVM with the given status code.

```scala
sys.error("something went wrong")
```
Throws a RuntimeException with the given message.

```scala
val x: Option[Int] = Some(42)
```
Wraps a value in an Option.

```scala
val x: Option[Int] = None
```
Represents the absence of a value.

```scala
val result: Either[String, Int] = Left("error")
```
Creates the left (typically error) side of an Either.

```scala
val result: Either[String, Int] = Right(42)
```
Creates the right (typically success) side of an Either.

```scala
val empty: List[Int] = Nil
val list = 1 :: 2 :: 3 :: Nil
```
The empty list.

```scala
val xs = List(1, 2, 3)
val ys = List.empty[String]
```
Creates an immutable linked list.

```scala
val m = Map("a" -> 1, "b" -> 2)
```
Creates an immutable map.

```scala
val s = Set(1, 2, 3)
```
Creates an immutable set.

```scala
val v = Vector(1, 2, 3)
```
Creates an immutable indexed sequence with efficient random access.

```scala
val arr = Array(1, 2, 3)
val arr2 = new Array[Int](10)
```
Creates a mutable indexed sequence backed by a Java array.

```scala
val opt: Option[Int] = Option(maybeNull)
opt.getOrElse(0)
```
Represents an optional value: either Some(value) or None.

```scala
import scala.util.Try
val result = Try(riskyOperation())
```
Wraps a computation that may throw, yielding Success or Failure.

```scala
import scala.concurrent.Future
import scala.concurrent.ExecutionContext.Implicits.global
val f = Future { expensiveComputation() }
```
Represents an asynchronous computation.

```scala
import scala.concurrent.Promise
val p = Promise[Int]()
p.success(42)
p.future
```
A writable, single-assignment container that completes a Future.

```scala
val s = Stream.from(1)
```
Lazily evaluated stream (deprecated in Scala 2.13, use LazyList).

```scala
val naturals = LazyList.from(1)
val fibs: LazyList[Int] = 0 #:: 1 #:: fibs.zip(fibs.tail).map(_ + _)
```
Lazily evaluated immutable linked list (replaces Stream in 2.13+).

```scala
val r = 1 to 10
val r2 = 0 until 100 by 2
```
Represents an ordered sequence of integers.

```scala
val s: Seq[Int] = Seq(1, 2, 3)
```
General sequence type. Default implementation is List.

```scala
val is: IndexedSeq[Int] = IndexedSeq(1, 2, 3)
```
A sequence with efficient indexed access. Default implementation is Vector.

```scala
val it = Iterator(1, 2, 3)
while (it.hasNext) println(it.next())
```
A one-time traversable collection of elements.

```scala
val t = (1, "hello", true)
val (a, b, c) = t
```
Heterogeneous fixed-size collection.

```scala
@main def run(): Unit =
  println("Hello, World!")
```
Scala 3 main method entry point.

```scala
case class Person(name: String, age: Int)
```
Defines an immutable data class with auto-generated equals, hashCode, toString, copy, and unapply.

completions.label: sealed trait ADT

documentation.value:
```scala
sealed trait Shape
case class Circle(radius: Double) extends Shape
case class Rectangle(w: Double, h: Double) extends Shape
```
Algebraic data type pattern.

```scala
for {
  x <- xs
  y <- ys
  if x > 0
} yield (x, y)
```
Monadic for-comprehension.

```scala
value match {
  case Some(x) => x
  case None    => default
}
```
Exhaustive pattern matching.

```scala
extension (s: String)
  def greet: String = s"Hello, $s!"
  def exclaim: String = s + "!"
```
Scala 3 extension methods.

```scala
given intOrd: Ordering[Int] with
  def compare(x: Int, y: Int) = x - y

def sort[T](xs: List[T])(using ord: Ordering[T]) = xs.sorted
```
Scala 3 contextual abstractions.

completions.label: Future with map/flatMap

documentation.value:
```scala
import scala.concurrent.Future
import scala.concurrent.ExecutionContext.Implicits.global

val result = for {
  a <- Future(computeA())
  b <- Future(computeB(a))
} yield combine(a, b)
```
Composing Futures with for-comprehension.

completions.label: actor (Akka)

documentation.value:
```scala
case object Singleton
```
Defines a singleton case object, often used in ADTs.

```scala
implicit val ec: ExecutionContext = ExecutionContext.global
```
Provides an implicit value.

```scala
implicit def intToString(i: Int): String = i.toString
```
Defines an implicit conversion.

```scala
trait Repository[T] {
  def findById(id: Long): Option[T]
  def findAll: List[T]
  def save(entity: T): Unit
}
```
Trait with abstract method signatures.

```scala
enum Color(val rgb: Int) {
  case Red   extends Color(0xFF0000)
  case Green extends Color(0x00FF00)
  case Blue  extends Color(0x0000FF)
}
```
Parameterized enum in Scala 3.

documentation.value:
```scala
trait Show[A]:
  extension (a: A) def show: String

given Show[Int] with
  extension (i: Int) def show: String = i.toString
```
Type class pattern in Scala 3.

completions.insertText:
trait ${1:TypeClass}[${2:A}]:
  extension (a: ${2:A}) def ${3:method}: ${4:String}

given ${1:TypeClass}[${5:ConcreteType}] with
  extension (a: ${5:ConcreteType}) def ${3:method}: ${4:String} = $0

documentation.value:
```scala
import scala.util.Success
result match { case Success(v) => v }
```
Represents the successful result of a Try.

```scala
import scala.util.Failure
result match { case Failure(e) => handle(e) }
```
Represents a failed Try containing an exception.

```scala
import scala.collection.mutable.ListBuffer
val buf = ListBuffer(1, 2, 3)
buf += 4
```
A mutable buffer backed by a list.

```scala
import scala.collection.mutable.ArrayBuffer
val buf = ArrayBuffer(1, 2, 3)
buf += 4
```
A mutable buffer backed by an array.

```scala
import scala.collection.mutable.HashMap
val m = HashMap("a" -> 1, "b" -> 2)
```
A mutable map backed by a hash table.

```scala
import scala.collection.immutable.TreeMap
val m = TreeMap("a" -> 1, "b" -> 2)
```
An immutable sorted map backed by a red-black tree.

```scala
import scala.collection.immutable.SortedSet
val s = SortedSet(3, 1, 2)
```
An immutable sorted set.

```scala
import scala.collection.immutable.Queue
val q = Queue(1, 2, 3)
```
An immutable FIFO queue.

```scala
val s = List(1, 2, 3) // use List as stack
val head :: tail = s
```
Use List as a stack with :: and pattern matching.

```scala
import scala.util.Using
Using(new FileReader("file.txt")) { reader =>
  reader.read()
}
```
Automatically manages resource cleanup.

```scala
import scala.io.Source
val lines = Source.fromFile("file.txt").getLines().toList
```
Reads data from files, URLs, or other sources.

```scala
import scala.concurrent.Await
import scala.concurrent.duration._
val result = Await.result(future, 10.seconds)
```
Blocks until a Future completes.

```scala
import scala.concurrent.duration._
val d = 5.seconds
val d2 = Duration(10, MILLISECONDS)
```
Represents a time duration.

```scala
import scala.collection.mutable
val m = mutable.Map("a" -> 1)
m += ("b" -> 2)
```
A mutable map.

```scala
import scala.collection.mutable
val s = mutable.Set(1, 2, 3)
s += 4
```
A mutable set.

```scala
val sb = new StringBuilder
sb.append("Hello")
sb.append(" World")
sb.toString
```
Efficiently builds strings from parts.

```scala
import scala.util.matching.Regex
val pattern = """(\d+)""".r
pattern.findFirstIn("abc 123")
```
Regular expression pattern.

```scala
import scala.reflect.ClassTag
def create[T: ClassTag]: Array[T] = new Array[T](10)
```
Provides runtime class information for generic types.

```scala
val pf: PartialFunction[Int, String] = {
  case 1 => "one"
  case 2 => "two"
}
```
A function defined only for certain inputs.

```scala
implicit val ord: Ordering[Person] =
  Ordering.by(_.name)
```
Type class for ordering/comparison.

```scala
def sum[T: Numeric](xs: List[T]): T = xs.sum
```
Type class for numeric operations.
